title: Supreme Court Hears Arguments on Trump's Move to Strip TPS from 350,000 Haitians and 6,000 Syrians
detail: The U.S. Supreme Court on April 29, 2026 heard oral arguments in two consolidated cases — Mullin v. Dahlia Doe (concerning ~350,000 Haitian TPS holders) and Trump v. Miot (concerning ~6,000 Syrian TPS holders) — on the Trump administration's authority to terminate Temporary Protected Status for migrants from those countries. The core legal question before the Court is whether federal courts have jurisdiction to review the executive branch's decision to terminate TPS designations, or whether such decisions are entirely within executive discretion and unreviewable. The Trump administration argues that TPS terminations are foreign policy and immigration enforcement judgments that courts cannot second-guess; civil liberties groups counter that the INA requires judicial review when fundamental rights are at stake. During arguments, Justice Kagan asked whether the administration's interpretation would allow TPS to be terminated 'for any reason or no reason at all,' while Justice Thomas probed the limits of congressional delegation. The cases are expected to produce a ruling by late June 2026. TPS holders from Haiti and Syria have been in the U.S. under these protections for over a decade; termination would trigger removal proceedings for hundreds of thousands of people.

media.caption: Supreme Court hears arguments on Trump's effort to terminate TPS for 350K Haitians and 6K Syrians, April 29, 2026

title: Iran War Day 61: Trump Warns Iran to 'Get Smart Soon'; UAE OPEC Exit Compounds Oil Market Pressure
detail: On Day 61 of the US-Iran war (April 29, 2026), President Trump posted on Truth Social warning Iran it had 'better get smart soon or there will be nothing left to negotiate over' — the sharpest public ultimatum since the ceasefire extension on April 21. The Strait of Hormuz ceasefire remained technically in effect but diplomacy was fully frozen: no second Islamabad round was scheduled, VP Vance's planned trip remained on hold, and Iran's Foreign Ministry said there was 'nothing to discuss' until the US lifted its naval blockade without preconditions. The UAE's announced exit from OPEC (confirmed April 28) continued to ripple through energy markets, with Brent crude trading around $97–99/barrel. Al Jazeera's live coverage documented no significant military movements during the day, but IRGC units in the Persian Gulf were reported on heightened alert posture. The ceasefire extended to this day on Pakistani mediation, with Islamabad still pressing both sides to return to talks. Trump simultaneously faced a record-low 34% approval rating as economic discontent and Iran war costs weighed on public opinion.

media.caption: Iran war Day 61: Trump warns Iran to 'get smart soon' as UAE OPEC exit deepens oil market pressure
